subject: Computer Science
coverageNote: Form directories exist only where teaching-module source PDFs were ingested. Missing forms must resolve as known-gap, not empty objects.

lesson.title: 2: DATA REPRESENTATION
topics: Data representation refers to the way data such as numbers, text, images, and sound are stored and
processed in a computer. Since computers only understand binary, different number systems and coding
schemes are used. This topic introduces learners to binary, denary, octal, and hexadecimal number
systems, and how text is represented using coding systems like ASCII.
activities: Classroom arranged for group work with space to create posters.
Clear view of board/projector for class discussion and comparison table.
Optional: computers or tablets for reference or digital base charts.
Projector/board to display examples of number systems.
assessment: Scenario: Your younger cousin in Chipata sees you working on a computer at a community centre
and asks, “Bakamba (Elder), I heard that computers only understand 0 and 1. But we are learning
about numbers like 10, 25, and 100 in school. How can a computer work with many school numbers
if it only knows two digits?”
(Binary/Denary) Explain to your cousin how the computer takes the number he sees (like 5)
